title: • Today's Intention: I will perform my daily work with excellence, but
content:
• Today's Intention: I will perform my daily work with excellence, but hold onto an inner freedom from the results.
    • NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "Business was a game, the rules of which he learned, the excitement of which he enjoyed, but his real self was not concerned."
    • Reflection: When did I feel "at play" today, even during work or chores? When did I feel burdened? What was the difference?
    Day 12
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "He learned many things from her rosy, clever mouth... He, who was still a boy in love... was taught by her that one cannot take pleasure without giving pleasure, and that every gesture, every caress, every touch, every glance... has its secret, which he who knows it can discover."
    • Today's Intention: I will seek a moment of genuine connection with someone today, beyond surface-level conversation.
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "They were like two players who were fond of each other, who kissed each other, but who were nevertheless not united. Their real life was somewhere else, far away."
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 16 (chunk 16/29)
